questions.question: A parabola has vertex at (2, −3) and focus at (2, 1). Write the equation of the parabola in standard form.
questions.explanation: The vertex and focus have the same x-coordinate, so the axis is vertical. Distance from vertex to focus is p = 4, so 4p = 16. Standard form for vertical parabola: (x − h)² = 4p(y − k).

questions.question: An ellipse has foci at (−5, 0) and (5, 0), and the sum of distances from any point on the ellipse to the two foci is 26. Find the equation of the ellipse.
questions.explanation: 2a = 26, so a = 13. Distance between foci is 2c = 10, so c = 5. Then b² = a² − c² = 169 − 25 = 144. Standard form: x²/a² + y²/b² = 1.

questions.question: A hyperbola has vertices at (0, ±6) and foci at (0, ±10). Which conic equation matches this hyperbola?
questions.explanation: Vertical transverse axis (y-term positive). a = 6, c = 10, so b² = c² − a² = 100 − 36 = 64. Standard form: y²/a² − x²/b² = 1.

questions.question: A parabola has equation y² = −12x. What are the coordinates of its focus and the equation of its directrix?
questions.explanation: Compare y² = −12x to y² = 4px: 4p = −12, so p = −3. Focus is at (p, 0) = (−3, 0) and directrix is the vertical line x = −p = 3.

questions.question: An ellipse has equation (x − 1)²/25 + (y + 2)²/9 = 1. A point P lies on the ellipse. If the distance from P to the left focus is 8, what is the distance from P to the right focus?
questions.explanation: a² = 25, b² = 9, so c² = 25 − 9 = 16, giving c = 4. Foci are at (1 ± 4, −2). By definition, sum of distances from P to both foci equals 2a = 10. If d₁ = 8, then d₂ = 10 − 8 = 2.

questions.question: A hyperbola has center at the origin, a vertical transverse axis, and asymptotes y = ±(3/2)x. If one vertex is at (0, 4), find the equation of the hyperbola.
questions.explanation: Vertical hyperbola: y²/a² − x²/b² = 1. Vertex at (0, 4) gives a = 4. Asymptote slope b/a = 3/2, so b = 6. Equation: y²/16 − x²/36 = 1.
